segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Labyrinths have existed for well over 4,000 years.
segments.zh: 迷宫已经存在了超过4000年。在土耳其、爱尔兰、希腊和印度等不同地区都发现了迷宫和迷宫符号。迷宫有各种设计，但它们的共同点是一条蜿蜒的螺旋路径通向中心区域。入口处有一个起点，目标是到达中心区域。在迷宫中穿行需要经历许多曲折，但不可能迷路，因为只有一条路。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Labyrinths and labyrinthine symbols have been found in regions as diverse as modern-day Turkey, Ireland, Greece, and India.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There are various designs of labyrinth but what they all have in common is a winding spiral path which leads to a central area.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There is one starting point at the entrance and the goal is to reach the central area.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Finding your way through a labyrinth involves many twists and turns, but it's not possible to get lost as there is only one single path.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In modern times, the word labyrinth has taken on a different meaning and is often used as a synonym for a maze.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A maze is quite different as it is a kind of puzzle with an intricate network of paths.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Mazes became fashionable in the 15th and 16th centuries in Europe, and can still be found in the gardens of great houses and palaces.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The paths are usually surrounded by thick, high hedges so that it's not possible to see over them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Entering a maze usually involves getting lost a few times before using logic to work out the pattern and find your way to the centre and then out again.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There are lots of dead ends and paths which lead you back to where you started.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The word 'maze' is believed to come from a Scandinavian word for a state of confusion.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This is where the word 'amazing' comes from.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Labyrinths, on the other hand, have a very different function.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Although people now often refer to things they find complicated as labyrinths, this is not how they were seen in the past.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The winding spiral of the labyrinth has been used for centuries as a metaphor for life's journey.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It served as a spiritual reminder that there is purpose and meaning to our lives and helped to give people a sense of direction.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Labyrinths are thought to encourage a feeling of calm and have been used as a meditation and prayer tool in many cultures over many centuries.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The earliest examples of the labyrinth spiral pattern have been found carved into stone, from Sardinia to Scandinavia, from Arizona to India to Africa.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In Europe, these spiral carvings date from the late Bronze Age.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The Native American Pima tribe wove baskets with a circular labyrinth design that depicted their own cosmology.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In Ancient Greece, the labyrinth spiral was used on coins around four thousand years ago.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Labyrinths made of mosaics were commonly found in bathhouses, villas and tombs throughout the Roman Empire.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In Northern Europe, there were actual physical labyrinths designed for walking on.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: These were cut into the turf or grass, usually in a circular pattern.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The origin of these walking labyrinths remains unclear, but they were probably used for fertility rites which may date back thousands of years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Eleven examples of turf labyrinths survive today, including the largest one at Saffron Walden, England, which used to have a large tree in the middle of it.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: More recently labyrinths have experienced something of a revival.
segments.zh: 最近，迷宫经历了一定程度的复兴。有些人认为走迷宫能促进疗愈和正念，还有人相信它在情感和身体上的益处，包括减缓呼吸、恢复平衡感和视角。这个想法变得如此流行，以至于近年来迷宫被铺设在水疗中心、健康中心甚至监狱的地板上。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Some believe that walking a labyrinth promotes healing and mindfulness, and there are those who believe in its emotional and physical benefits, which include slower breathing and a restored sense of balance and perspective.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This idea has become so popular that labyrinths have been laid into the floors of spas, wellness centres and even prisons in recent years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A pamphlet at Colorado Children's Hospital informs patients that 'walking a labyrinth can often calm people in the midst of a crisis'.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And apparently, it's not only patients who benefit.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Many visitors find walking a labyrinth less stressful than sitting in a corridor or waiting room.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Some doctors even walk the labyrinth during their breaks.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In some hospitals, patients who can't walk can have a paper 'finger labyrinth' brought to their bed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The science behind the theory is a little sketchy, but there are dozens of small-scale studies which support claims about the benefits of labyrinths.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For example, one study found that walking a labyrinth provided 'short-term calming, relaxation, and relief from anxiety' for Alzheimer's patients.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So, what is it about labyrinths that makes their appeal so universal?
segments.zh: 那么，迷宫究竟有什么魅力，能让它如此普遍地吸引人呢？嗯……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well ...

pairs.note: 原文中出现了maze，与题干Mazes对应，注意单复数不影响定位。
a type of 与 a kind of 是同义替换，表示“一种”。
原文直接出现puzzle，与答案一致。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Mazes是一种什么，原文中明确说'A maze is quite different as it is a kind of puzzle'，因此定位到puzzle。

items.prompt: is needed to navigate through a maze

pairs.note: 原文用'using'表示'需要用到'，与题干'is needed'同义替换。

pairs.p: find your way to the centre and then out again
pairs.note: 题干'navigate through a maze'被解释为'找到去中心再出来的路'，是具体化表述。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'using logic to work out the pattern and find your way to the centre and then out again'，其中'using logic'对应题干'is needed'，'find your way...'对应'navigate through a maze'，因此答案应为'logic'。

items.prompt: the word 'maze' is derived from a word meaning a feeling of

pairs.note: 题干和原文都是maze，直接对应。
derived from 和 come from 都表示‘来源于’。
feeling 和 state 在此语境下都指‘状态’。
题干和原文都是confusion，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句为‘The word 'maze' is believed to come from a Scandinavian word for a state of confusion.’，其中‘come from’替换‘derived from’，‘a state of’替换‘a feeling of’，‘confusion’直接对应。

items.prompt: they have frequently been used in and prayer

pairs.note: 'frequently been used in' 对应 'have been used as a'，表示用途。
'and prayer' 直接出现在原文中，但题干缺少 'meditation'，答案正是 'meditation'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句最后一句提到 'have been used as a meditation and prayer tool'，其中 'meditation' 是答案，题干 'frequently been used in and prayer' 对应了 'used as a ... and prayer'，通过 'prayer' 定位。

items.prompt: Ancient carvings on have been found across many cultures

pairs.note: 题干说'Ancient carvings on...'，原文说'carved into stone'，carving 和 carved 是动词名词转换，stone 是材料。
题干介词'on'对应原文介词'into'，都表示雕刻在石头上。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'Ancient carvings on stone'，定位句'carved into stone'直接对应，注意carving和carved的动词名词替换，以及介词on和into的替换。

items.prompt: Ancient Greeks used the symbol on

pairs.note: Greeks 对应 Greece，都是指古希腊人/地区。
主动语态 used on 变为被动语态 was used on，意思相同。
原词重现，直接定位答案。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句 'In Ancient Greece, the labyrinth spiral was used on coins around four thousand years ago.' 中，Ancient Greece 对应题干 Ancient Greeks，was used on 对应 used the symbol on，coins 原词出现，因此答案就是 coins。

items.prompt: The largest surviving example of a turf labyrinth once had a big at its centre

pairs.note: 原文用'largest one'指代'largest surviving example'
原文直接出现'turf labyrinths'，与题干相同

pairs.q: had a big at its centre
pairs.p: used to have a large tree in the middle of it
pairs.note: 原文用'used to have a large tree in the middle of it'解释'had a big at its centre'

paraphrase.explain: 定位句最后一句提到'the largest one at Saffron Walden, England, which used to have a large tree in the middle of it'，与题干'largest surviving example of a turf labyrinth once had a big at its centre'完全对应，其中'largest one'替换'largest surviving example'，'large tree'替换'big'。

items.prompt: walking a maze can reduce a person's rate

pairs.note: 题干说降低速率，原文说更慢的呼吸，是同一意思的转述。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 walking a labyrinth 的好处包括 slower breathing，与题干 walking a maze can reduce a person's rate 对应，rate 指呼吸速率。

items.prompt: patients who can't walk can use 'finger labyrinths' made from

pairs.note: 原文完全相同的短语，直接定位
原文是单数，题干是复数，但核心词相同
原文中'paper'直接修饰'finger labyrinth'，表示材质

paraphrase.explain: 定位句末尾提到'patients who can't walk can have a paper finger labyrinth'，与题干'patients who can't walk can use finger labyrinths made from'完全对应，答案就是'paper'。

items.prompt: research has shown that Alzheimer's sufferers experience less

pairs.note: 题干说'经历更少焦虑'，原文说'缓解焦虑'，意思一致但表达不同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问阿尔茨海默症患者经历更少的什么，定位句末尾提到'relief from anxiety'，即缓解焦虑，所以答案是anxiety。
